name: Robot Vacuum Cleaner
tagline: Find the perfect robot vacuum for your home
intro: Robot vacuums have evolved massively — from basic bump-and-go pucks to AI-powered cleaning machines with self-emptying bases, mopping, and obstacle avoidance. Prices range from £150 to £1,500+. The right one depends on your home, your floors, your pets, and how hands-off you want to be.

thingsToConsider.title: Navigation Technology
thingsToConsider.summary: How the robot maps and moves around your home. The biggest factor in cleaning quality.
thingsToConsider.detail: Cheap robots bounce randomly (bump-and-go). Mid-range use gyroscopes or cameras. Premium use LiDAR (laser mapping) which creates accurate floor plans and cleans systematically. LiDAR models clean faster, miss fewer spots, and let you set no-go zones via the app.
thingsToConsider.recommendation: LiDAR is worth it if you can afford it. Camera-based (vSLAM) is a solid middle ground. Avoid random navigation for anything bigger than a studio flat.

thingsToConsider.title: Suction Power (Pa)
thingsToConsider.summary: How strongly the robot pulls up dirt. Measured in Pascals (Pa).
thingsToConsider.detail: Budget: 2,000-4,000 Pa. Mid-range: 5,000-8,000 Pa. Premium: 10,000-15,000+ Pa. Higher suction picks up more from carpets and gets embedded pet hair. Most robots adjust suction automatically — boosting on carpet, quieter on hard floors.
thingsToConsider.recommendation: If you have mostly hard floors, 4,000+ Pa is fine. Carpets or pets: aim for 6,000+ Pa. Deep pile carpet: 10,000+ Pa.

thingsToConsider.title: Self-Emptying Base
thingsToConsider.summary: A dock that automatically empties the robot's dustbin into a larger bag/container.
thingsToConsider.detail: Without self-emptying, you need to empty the small onboard dustbin after every 1-2 cleans. Self-emptying bases hold 30-60 days of dust. Some use bags (£3-5 each, replaced monthly), some are bagless. This is the #1 convenience feature.
thingsToConsider.recommendation: Strongly recommended if you want a hands-off experience. The price premium (£100-200) is worth it for most people.

thingsToConsider.title: Mopping Capability
thingsToConsider.summary: Many modern robot vacuums can also mop hard floors.
thingsToConsider.detail: Basic mopping: a damp pad dragged behind (barely effective). Vibrating/sonic mopping: pad vibrates to scrub (much better). Rotating dual pads: two spinning mop pads with downward pressure (best). Some premium models lift the mop pads when on carpet to avoid wetting it. Hot water washing of mop pads in the base station is a top-tier feature.
thingsToConsider.recommendation: If you have lots of hard floors, look for vibrating or rotating mop pads. Basic drag-mopping is barely worth having.

thingsToConsider.title: Obstacle Avoidance
thingsToConsider.summary: How well the robot avoids cables, shoes, pet toys, and other floor clutter.
thingsToConsider.detail: Cheap robots have no obstacle avoidance — they'll eat cables and get stuck on socks. Mid-range use cameras or structured light to detect and avoid objects. Premium models use AI-powered cameras that can identify specific objects (shoes, cables, pet waste) and avoid them.
thingsToConsider.recommendation: Essential if you have kids, pets, or don't want to baby-proof your floors before every clean.

thingsToConsider.title: Battery Life & Coverage
thingsToConsider.summary: How much area the robot can clean on a single charge.
thingsToConsider.detail: Budget: 90-120 mins (60-80m²). Mid-range: 150-180 mins (120-180m²). Premium: 180-300 mins (200-300m²). Good robots will return to charge and resume cleaning automatically (recharge & resume). Most UK homes are well within range of mid-range models.
thingsToConsider.recommendation: For a typical 3-bed semi (~80m²), any decent robot will handle it in one charge. Larger homes (150m²+) need recharge & resume.

thingsToConsider.title: Noise Level
thingsToConsider.summary: How loud the robot is while cleaning.
thingsToConsider.detail: Typical range: 55-75 dB. 55 dB is like a quiet conversation. 70+ dB is like a standard vacuum. Most robots are quieter than a traditional vacuum but louder than you'd expect. Many have a 'quiet' mode that reduces suction but drops noise significantly.
thingsToConsider.recommendation: If you plan to run it while home or at night, check the quiet mode noise level. If you run it while out, noise doesn't matter.

thingsToConsider.title: App & Smart Home Integration
thingsToConsider.summary: How you control the robot — app features, Alexa/Google support, scheduling.
thingsToConsider.detail: All decent robots have apps for scheduling, maps, and controls. Look for: room-specific cleaning, no-go zones, cleaning history, suction/water level per room. Alexa/Google Home support lets you voice-start cleaning. Some work with Matter/HomeKit.
thingsToConsider.recommendation: The app experience varies wildly. Roborock, Ecovacs, and iRobot have the best apps. Check reviews for app quality.

thingsToConsider.title: Maintenance & Running Costs
thingsToConsider.summary: Filters, brushes, mop pads, and dust bags need replacing regularly.
thingsToConsider.detail: Typical annual costs: Main brush (£10-20, every 6-12 months), side brushes (£5-10, every 3-6 months), filter (£8-15, every 2-4 months), dust bags (£3-5 each, monthly with self-empty). Third-party alternatives are much cheaper than official parts. Budget £30-60/year for consumables.
thingsToConsider.recommendation: Check availability and price of replacement parts before buying. Popular brands (Roborock, Ecovacs, iRobot) have cheap third-party parts on Amazon.

jargon.meaning: Light Detection and Ranging — a spinning laser sensor that creates accurate 3D maps of your home. The gold standard for robot vacuum navigation.
Visual Simultaneous Localization and Mapping — uses a camera to navigate. Cheaper than LiDAR but can struggle in low light.
Unit of suction power. Higher = stronger suction. 5,000 Pa is decent, 10,000+ Pa is powerful.
Robot returns to dock when battery is low, charges up, then continues cleaning where it left off. Essential for large homes.
Areas you mark in the app where the robot won't go. Virtual walls without physical barriers.
High-Efficiency Particulate Air filter — captures 99.97% of particles. Important for allergy sufferers.
Robot automatically raises the mop pads when it detects carpet, so it doesn't wet your rugs.
Two rubber extractors instead of a bristle brush. Better for pet hair — hair doesn't tangle around them.
The base station washes the mop pads with hot water after cleaning. Keeps them hygienic and removes grease.
Noise measurement. 50 dB = quiet office. 60 dB = normal conversation. 70 dB = shower running.
Robot automatically increases suction when it detects carpet for deeper cleaning.
Projects infrared dots to detect objects in 3D. Used for obstacle avoidance.
Where the robot lives when not cleaning. Basic docks just charge. Premium docks empty dust, wash mops, refill water, and dry mop pads.

questions.question: What type of floors do you mostly have?
How big is the area you want cleaned?
How hands-off do you want it?

options.label: Fully autonomous
options.desc: Just want it to work without thinking

products.name: Roborock Q5 Pro+
products.bestFor: Best budget pick with self-emptying. Great for simple homes without too much floor clutter.

products.name: Eufy X10 Pro Omni
products.bestFor: Outstanding mid-range all-rounder. Does everything well at a competitive price.

products.name: Roborock Q Revo MaxV
products.bestFor: Best mid-range vacuum+mop combo. Hot water mop washing keeps mop pads hygienic.

products.name: Dreame L20 Ultra
features: Self-emptying + hot water mop wash + hot air drying
products.bestFor: Does everything and does it well. The auto-extending mop reaches edges other robots miss.

products.name: Roborock S8 MaxV Ultra
features: Self-emptying + hot water mop wash + hot air drying
products.bestFor: The best robot vacuum you can buy. If you want the absolute top-tier and budget isn't an issue.

products.name: iRobot Roomba Combo J9+
products.bestFor: Best if you want a trusted Western brand with excellent app and customer support. Popular in the US/UK.

products.name: Ecovacs Deebot T30 Omni
features: Self-empty + hot water mop wash + hot air dry
missing: Ecovacs app less intuitive than Roborock
products.bestFor: Exceptional suction power at a competitive price. The ZeroTangle brush is genuinely anti-tangle.

products.name: Xiaomi Robot Vacuum X20+
products.bestFor: Great value from a trusted brand. Good for tidy homes where obstacle avoidance isn't needed.

budget.label: Entry Level
budget.description: Basic navigation, decent cleaning. Good for simple homes.

mid.label: Sweet Spot
mid.description: LiDAR navigation, self-emptying, some mopping. Best value for most people.

premium.label: Premium
premium.description: AI obstacle avoidance, great mop + vacuum, all the bells and whistles.

flagship.label: Flagship
flagship.description: The absolute best. Maximum suction, best navigation, most autonomous.
